data.previous_feedback:
here are some feedbacks:
- The current result only lists high-level workflow steps and does not implement the required email notification module.
- We need a Python module/class that can send emails via SMTP at specified workflow stages, with configurable templates and SMTP settings.

here are the changes:
```python
import smtplib
from email.mime.text import MIMEText
from email.mime.multipart import MIMEMultipart

class EmailNotifier:
    """
    A module to send notification emails at specified workflow stages.
    """

    def __init__(self, smtp_server, smtp_port, smtp_user, smtp_password, use_tls=True):
        self.smtp_server = smtp_server
        self.smtp_port = smtp_port
        self.smtp_user = smtp_user
        self.smtp_password = smtp_password
        self.use_tls = use_tls

        # Define email templates per workflow stage
        self.templates = {
            "job_description_analyzed": {
                "subject": "Job Description Analysis Complete",
                "body": (
                    "Hello {name},\n\n"
                    "The job description for '{job_title}' has been analyzed.\n"
                    "Next, we'll analyze your resume.\n\n"
                    "Best regards,\n"
                    "Recruitment Team"
                )
            },
            "resume_analyzed": {
                "subject": "Resume Analysis Complete",
                "body": (
                    "Hello {name},\n\n"
                    "We have completed analyzing your resume for '{job_title}'.\n"
                    "Up next: technical interview scheduling.\n\n"
                    "Best regards,\n"
                    "Recruitment Team"
                )
            },
            "technical_interview_scheduled": {
                "subject": "Technical Interview Scheduled",
                "body": (
                    "Hello {name},\n\n"
                    "Your technical interview for '{job_title}' is scheduled on {interview_date}.\n"
                    "Please confirm your availability.\n\n"
                    "Best regards,\n"
                    "Recruitment Team"
                )
            },
            "score_submitted": {
                "subject": "Interview Score Submitted",
                "body": (
                    "Hello {name},\n\n"
                    "Your interview has been scored.\n"
                    "Score: {score}\n\n"
                    "Thank you for your time.\n\n"
                    "Best regards,\n"
                    "Recruitment Team"
                )
            },
            "final_evaluation": {
                "subject": "Final Evaluation Report",
                "body": (
                    "Hello {name},\n\n"
                    "The final evaluation for '{job_title}' is complete.\n"
                    "Result: {result}\n\n"
                    "Best regards,\n"
                    "Recruitment Team"
                )
            }
        }

    def send_email(self, recipient, subject, body, sender=None):
        """
        Sends an email using SMTP.
        """
        sender = sender or self.smtp_user
        msg = MIMEMultipart()
        msg["From"] = sender
        msg["To"] = recipient
        msg["Subject"] = subject
        msg.attach(MIMEText(body, "plain"))

        server = smtplib.SMTP(self.smtp_server, self.smtp_port)
        try:
            if self.use_tls:
                server.starttls()
            server.login(self.smtp_user, self.smtp_password)
            server.sendmail(sender, recipient, msg.as_string())
        finally:
            server.quit()

    def notify(self, stage, recipient_email, context):
        """
        Send a notification email for the given workflow stage.
        `stage` must be one of the keys in self.templates.
        `context` is a dict with template variables (e.g., name, job_title).
        """
        template = self.templates.get(stage)
        if not template:
            raise ValueError(f"No email template defined for stage '{stage}'")

        subject = template["subject"]
        body = template["body"].format(**context)
        self.send_email(recipient_email, subject, body)


# Example usage
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # SMTP configuration (replace with real credentials in production)
    notifier = EmailNotifier(
        smtp_server="smtp.example.com",
        smtp_port=587,
        smtp_user="no-reply@example.com",
        smtp_password="securepassword",
        use_tls=True
    )

    # Notify candidate that job description analysis is done
    notifier.notify(
        stage="job_description_analyzed",
        recipient_email="candidate@example.com",
        context={
            "name": "Alice",
            "job_title": "Software Engineer"
        }
    )
```

import smtplib
from email.mime.text import MIMEText
from email.mime.multipart import MIMEMultipart

class EmailNotifier:
    """
    A module to send notification emails at specified workflow stages.
    """

    def __init__(self, smtp_server, smtp_port, smtp_user, smtp_password, use_tls=True):
        self.smtp_server = smtp_server
        self.smtp_port = smtp_port
        self.smtp_user = smtp_user
        self.smtp_password = smtp_password
        self.use_tls = use_tls

        # Define email templates per workflow stage
        self.templates = {
            "job_description_analyzed": {
                "subject": "Job Description Analysis Complete",
                "body": (
                    "Hello {name},\n\n"
                    "The job description for '{job_title}' has been analyzed.\n"
                    "Next, we'll analyze your resume.\n\n"
                    "Best regards,\n"
                    "Recruitment Team"
                )
            },
            "resume_analyzed": {
                "subject": "Resume Analysis Complete",
                "body": (
                    "Hello {name},\n\n"
                    "We have completed analyzing your resume for '{job_title}'.\n"
                    "Up next: technical interview scheduling.\n\n"
                    "Best regards,\n"
                    "Recruitment Team"
                )
            },
            "technical_interview_scheduled": {
                "subject": "Technical Interview Scheduled",
                "body": (
                    "Hello {name},\n\n"
                    "Your technical interview for '{job_title}' is scheduled on {interview_date}.\n"
                    "Please confirm your availability.\n\n"
                    "Best regards,\n"
                    "Recruitment Team"
                )
            },
            "score_submitted": {
                "subject": "Interview Score Submitted",
                "body": (
                    "Hello {name},\n\n"
                    "Your interview has been scored.\n"
                    "Score: {score}\n\n"
                    "Thank you for your time.\n\n"
                    "Best regards,\n"
                    "Recruitment Team"
                )
            },
            "final_evaluation": {
                "subject": "Final Evaluation Report",
                "body": (
                    "Hello {name},\n\n"
                    "The final evaluation for '{job_title}' is complete.\n"
                    "Result: {result}\n\n"
                    "Best regards,\n"
                    "Recruitment Team"
                )
            }
        }

    def send_email(self, recipient, subject, body, sender=None):
        """
        Sends an email using SMTP.
        """
        sender = sender or self.smtp_user
        msg = MIMEMultipart()
        msg["From"] = sender
        msg["To"] = recipient
        msg["Subject"] = subject
        msg.attach(MIMEText(body, "plain"))

        server = smtplib.SMTP(self.smtp_server, self.smtp_port)
        try:
            if self.use_tls:
                server.starttls()
            server.login(self.smtp_user, self.smtp_password)
            server.sendmail(sender, recipient, msg.as_string())
        finally:
            server.quit()

    def notify(self, stage, recipient_email, context):
        """
        Send a notification email for the given workflow stage.
        `stage` must be one of the keys in self.templates.
        `context` is a dict with template variables (e.g., name, job_title).
        """
        template = self.templates.get(stage)
        if not template:
            raise ValueError(f"No email template defined for stage '{stage}'")

        subject = template["subject"]
        body = template["body"].format(**context)
        self.send_email(recipient_email, subject, body)

import smtplib
from email.mime.text import MIMEText
from email.mime.multipart import MIMEMultipart

class EmailNotifier:
    """
    A module to send notification emails at specified workflow stages.
    """

    def __init__(self, smtp_server, smtp_port, smtp_user, smtp_password, use_tls=True):
        self.smtp_server = smtp_server
        self.smtp_port = smtp_port
        self.smtp_user = smtp_user
        self.smtp_password = smtp_password
        self.use_tls = use_tls

        # Define email templates per workflow stage
        self.templates = {
            "job_description_analyzed": {
                "subject": "Job Description Analysis Complete",
                "body": (
                    "Hello {name},\n\n"
                    "The job description for '{job_title}' has been analyzed.\n"
                    "Next, we'll analyze your resume.\n\n"
                    "Best regards,\n"
                    "Recruitment Team"
                )
            },
            "resume_analyzed": {
                "subject": "Resume Analysis Complete",
                "body": (
                    "Hello {name},\n\n"
                    "We have completed analyzing your resume for '{job_title}'.\n"
                    "Up next: technical interview scheduling.\n\n"
                    "Best regards,\n"
                    "Recruitment Team"
                )
            },
            "technical_interview_scheduled": {
                "subject": "Technical Interview Scheduled",
                "body": (
                    "Hello {name},\n\n"
                    "Your technical interview for '{job_title}' is scheduled on {interview_date}.\n"
                    "Please confirm your availability.\n\n"
                    "Best regards,\n"
                    "Recruitment Team"
                )
            },
            "score_submitted": {
                "subject": "Interview Score Submitted",
                "body": (
                    "Hello {name},\n\n"
                    "Your interview has been scored.\n"
                    "Score: {score}\n\n"
                    "Thank you for your time.\n\n"
                    "Best regards,\n"
                    "Recruitment Team"
                )
            },
            "final_evaluation": {
                "subject": "Final Evaluation Report",
                "body": (
                    "Hello {name},\n\n"
                    "The final evaluation for '{job_title}' is complete.\n"
                    "Result: {result}\n\n"
                    "Best regards,\n"
                    "Recruitment Team"
                )
            }
        }

    def send_email(self, recipient, subject, body, sender=None):
        """
        Sends an email using SMTP.
        """
        sender = sender or self.smtp_user
        msg = MIMEMultipart()
        msg["From"] = sender
        msg["To"] = recipient
        msg["Subject"] = subject
        msg.attach(MIMEText(body, "plain"))

        server = smtplib.SMTP(self.smtp_server, self.smtp_port)
        try:
            if self.use_tls:
                server.starttls()
            server.login(self.smtp_user, self.smtp_password)
            server.sendmail(sender, recipient, msg.as_string())
        finally:
            server.quit()

    def notify(self, stage, recipient_email, context):
        """
        Send a notification email for the given workflow stage.
        `stage` must be one of the keys in self.templates.
        `context` is a dict with template variables (e.g., name, job_title).
        """
        template = self.templates.get(stage)
        if not template:
            raise ValueError(f"No email template defined for stage '{stage}'")

        subject = template["subject"]
        body = template["body"].format(**context)
        self.send_email(recipient_email, subject, body)


# Example usage
if __name__ == "__main__":
    # SMTP configuration (replace with real credentials in production)
    notifier = EmailNotifier(
        smtp_server="smtp.example.com",
        smtp_port=587,
        smtp_user="no-reply@example.com",

        recipient_email="candidate@example.com",
        context={
            "name": "Alice",
            "job_title": "Software Engineer"
        }
    )
